destinations.evidence: The pool deadline extractor selects ABI field 5 for persistent-agent payloads and returns None when fewer than six ABI words are present, matching the 192-byte short-input rule.
The shared decoder routes 0x0820 through the full PersistentAgentRequest codec and returns its executor and TTL fields.

equivalence_argument: The port renames extract_two_phase_deadline to the pool-local phase2_deadline_offset helper, but it reads the same persistent-agent max_spawn_block word at index 5 and uses full-codec decoding for executor and TTL.
notes: The deadline path is owned by async-pool metadata while executor/TTL decoding is owned by the shared precompile codec.
rationale: The tests assert deadline extraction equals `Some(4000)` from field 5, short input returns `None`, and full-codec decoding preserves executor and TTL. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For persistent-agent (`0x0820`) payloads, `extract_two_phase_deadline` reads `max_spawn_block` from field 5 and returns `None` for inputs shorter than 192 bytes; `try_decode_precompile_data` returns the payload executor and TTL using the full codec.
suggested_falsification_tests: Encode a persistent-agent payload with a known `max_spawn_block`, executor, and TTL; assert extraction/decode match, then truncate below 192 bytes and assert no deadline is returned.
summary: Persistent-agent deadline and executor/TTL decoding are preserved.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Encode a persistent-agent payload with known max_spawn_block, executor, and TTL; assert field-5 deadline extraction and try_decode_precompile_data match, then truncate below 192 bytes and assert no deadline is returned.
title: Persistent-agent deadline and decode fields
